option.value: {"C:/Users/anton/Documents/GitHub/hillel":"{\"/homeworks/hw27/practice/index.html\":\"This file appears to be an HTML document that creates a simple contact management application using Bootstrap and JavaScript. It does not contain any defined classes or functions, but it does have the following elements:\\n\\n`\\u003cmain\\u003e`: The main content area of the page, which includes a container with a row and a column for the contact management interface.\\n\\n`\\u003cdiv data-contacts-alert\\u003e`: An alert message that is displayed when there are no contacts.\\n\\n`\\u003cul data-contacts-list\\u003e`: A list element that will display the user\\u0027s contacts.\\n\\n`\\u003cdiv class\\u003d\\\"modal fade\\\" id\\u003d\\\"addContactModal\\\"\\u003e`: A modal dialog that allows the user to add a new contact.\\n\\n`\\u003cdiv class\\u003d\\\"modal fade\\\" id\\u003d\\\"deleteContactModal\\\"\\u003e`: A modal dialog that allows the user to delete a contact.\\n\\n`\\u003cdiv class\\u003d\\\"toast-container position-fixed bottom-0 end-0 p-3\\\"\\u003e`: Two toast notifications that display when a contact is added or deleted.\\n\\n`\\u003cscript src\\u003d\\\"index.js\\\"\\u003e`: A reference to a JavaScript file that likely contains the logic for managing the contacts.\\n\\nOverall, this file sets up the structure and layout of a contact management application, with the functionality implemented in the referenced JavaScript file.\",\"/homeworks/hw32/scripts/modules/uiVariables.js\":\"This file does not contain any classes or functions. Instead, it exports an object `uiVariables` that contains references to various HTML elements on the page, such as a form, input fields, an error block, a button, and elements within a card. This object can be used to easily access and manipulate these UI elements throughout the application.\",\"/homeworks/hw17/js/indexOf.js\":\"This file does not contain any classes. It defines a single function:\\n\\n`indexOf`: This function returns the first index at which a given element can be found in the array, or -1 if it is not present. It works similarly to the built-in `Array.prototype.indexOf` method.\\n\\nThe file also includes a sample usage of the `indexOf` function with the `numbers` array.\",\"/homeworks/hw17/js/includes.js\":\"This file does not contain any classes. It defines a single function:\\n\\n`includes`: This function checks if the given array contains the specified element, starting from the specified index, and returns `true` if the element is found, otherwise `false`.\\n\\nThe file also demonstrates the usage of the `includes` function by calling it with different arguments and logging the results to the console.\",\"/homeworks/hw26/README.md\":\"This file does not contain any classes or functions. It is a description of a registration form with the following requirements:\\n\\n1. Collect a registration form with the following fields: email, password, confirm password, age (number), city (select), agree to terms (checkbox), \\\"Register\\\" button, and \\\"Clear\\\" button.\\n2. Implement live and submit validation for the form fields, including email format, password complexity, password confirmation, age range, and checkbox selection.\\n3. Serialize the form data into a JSON object and display it under the form upon submission.\\n4. Implement a \\\"Clear\\\" button that resets the form, clears the localStorage, and removes any UI errors and the JSON result.\\n5. Provide acceptance criteria, including the inability to submit the form with invalid data, the preservation of field values upon page reload, and the display of the JSON data after submission.\\n6. Bonus features include a password strength indicator and a \\\"Show/Hide Password\\\" functionality.\",\"/homeworks/hw34/styles/style.css\":\"There are no classes or functions in the provided document. The document appears to be an empty XML document, which does not contain any meaningful content.\",\"/homeworks/hw6/README.md\":\"This file does not contain any classes or functions. Instead, it provides a set of instructions for creating a user information \\\"survey\\\" that collects the user\\u0027s name, age, city, and their opinion on JavaScript. The collected information is then displayed in a single alert message.\",\"/homeworks/hw16/js/script.js\":\"This file does not contain any classes. It contains the following function:\\n\\n`removeElement`: This function removes the first occurrence of the specified item from the given array.\\n\\nThe file also declares a constant array `array` with the values `[1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, 7]`. The `removeElement` function is then called with the `array` and the value `5`, which removes the first occurrence of `5` from the array, resulting in the output `[1, 2, 3, 4, 6, 7]`.\",\"/homeworks/hw26/css/style.css\":\"This file contains CSS styles for a form and a section to display JSON data. It does not contain any classes or functions. The file sets the styles for the body, form, input fields, buttons, and a section to display JSON data.\\n\\nThe file sets the font, background color, and padding for the body. It also styles the form, including the maximum width, background color, padding, border radius, and box shadow. The input fields and select elements are styled with specific border, font, and focus styles. The error messages are styled with a specific color and font size. The submit and reset buttons are styled with different background colors and hover effects. Finally, the section to display JSON data is styled with a specific background color, font, and box shadow.\",\"/homeworks/hw18/README.md\":\"This file does not contain any classes or functions. Instead, it provides a set of requirements for two functions that should be implemented:\\n\\n1. A function that should work similarly to the `array.shift` method, which removes and returns the first element of an array.\\n2. A function that should work similarly to the `array.reverse` method, which reverses the order of the elements in an array.\\n\\nThe file also specifies that the implementation of these functions should not use any built-in array methods.\",\"/homeworks/hw32/scripts/modules/events.js\":\"This file does not contain any classes. It contains the following functions:\\n\\n`initFormEvents`: This function sets up an event listener for the form submission, which handles the validation and updating of the profile model and the UI.\\n\\n`initFreezeButton`: This function sets up an event listener for the freeze button, which allows the user to freeze the profile model and prevent further changes.\\n\\nThe file also imports variables and functions from other modules, such as `uiVariables`, `profileModel`, and `errorHandler`, which are used throughout the code.\",\"/homeworks/hw4/js/script.js\":\"This file does not contain any classes or functions. Instead, it is a simple JavaScript program that calculates the final price of a product after applying a discount. The program prompts the user to enter the product\\u0027s starting price and the discount percentage, and then it calculates and displays the final price with the discount applied.\",\"/homeworks/hw21/css/style.css\":\"There are no classes or functions in the provided document. The document appears to be an empty XML document with a `\\u003cdocument\\u003e` and `\\u003c/document\\u003e` tag, but no content within it.\",\"/homeworks/hw11/README.md\":\"This file does not contain any classes or functions. Instead, it provides a set of programming exercises that involve using ternary operators to perform various conditional checks and operations on variables. The exercises cover a range of scenarios, such as checking the value and type of a variable, performing arithmetic operations based on variable values, and using a switch-case statement to assign values to a variable based on a numeric input.\",\"/homeworks/hw28/scripts/modules/uiRenderer.js\":\"This file does not contain any classes. It contains the following functions:\\n\\n`uiContactsListHandler`: This function creates and manages a contacts list, including the ability to add and remove contacts.\\n\\n`createItemTemplate`: This function creates an HTML template for a single contact item, including the contact\\u0027s name, phone, and address, as well as a delete button.\\n\\n`addElement`: This function adds a new contact item to the contacts list and updates the UI accordingly.\\n\\n`removeElement`: This function removes a contact item from the contacts list and updates the UI accordingly.\\n\\n`listHandler`: This is an instance of the `uiContactsListHandler` function, which is exported as the default export of the file.\\n\\nOverall, this file provides a reusable UI component for managing a list of contacts, with functionality for adding and removing contacts.\",\"/homeworks/hw12/js/script.js\":\"This file does not contain any classes or functions. Instead, it contains several tasks that use loops and conditional statements to perform various operations. Here\\u0027s a summary of each task:\\n\\nTask 1: Generates a string of numbers from 20 to 30 with a step of 0.5 and logs it to the console.\\n\\nTask 2: Calculates the price in UAH for different amounts of USD (10 to 100 with a step of 10) and logs the results to the console.\\n\\nTask 3: Finds and logs all the perfect squares less than or equal to a given number (68) to the console.\\n\\nTask 4: Checks if a given number (23) is prime and logs the result (\\\"Yes\\\" or \\\"No\\\") to the console.\\n\\nTask 5: Checks if a given number (34) can be expressed as a power of 3 and logs the result (\\\"YES\\\" or \\\"NO\\\") to the console.\",\"/homeworks/hw17/css/style.css\":\"There are no classes or functions in the provided file. The file appears to be an empty XML document with a `\\u003cdocument\\u003e` tag, but no content within it.\",\"/homeworks/hw28/scripts/modules/events.js\":\"This file does not contain any classes. It contains the following functions:\\n\\n`initEvents`: This function sets up event listeners for various UI elements, such as the \\\"Add Contact\\\" button, the \\\"Add Contact\\\" form, and the \\\"Delete Contact\\\" buttons, and handles the corresponding actions, such as adding a new contact, deleting a contact, and displaying success/error messages.\\n\\nThe file also imports two objects, `validationRegExps` and `errorMessages`, from a separate module called `contactValidationConfig.js`. These objects are used for form validation within the `initEvents` function.\\n\\nOverall, this file is responsible for managing the user interactions and events related to the contact management functionality of the application.\",\"/homeworks/hw8/js/script2.js\":\"This file does not contain any classes or functions. Instead, it demonstrates the creation and manipulation of JavaScript arrays.\\n\\nThe file performs the following tasks:\\n\\n1. Declares two arrays, `a` and `b`, with values `[1, 2, 3]` and `[4, 5]`, respectively.\\n2. Creates a new array `c` by concatenating the elements of `a` and `b`.\\n3. Logs the contents of the `c` array to the console.\",\"/homeworks/hw8/index.html\":\"This HTML file does not contain any classes or functions. It is a basic HTML document that includes four JavaScript files: `script1.js`, `script2.js`, `script3.js`, and `script4.js`. The purpose of this HTML file is to load and execute the code from these JavaScript files.\",\"/homeworks/hw17/js/lastIndexOf.js\":\"This file does not contain any classes. It defines a single function:\\n\\n`lastIndexOf`: This function returns the last index of a given element in an array, or -1 if the element is not found. The search starts from the specified index and moves backwards. If no index is provided, the search starts from the end of the array.\\n\\nThe file also demonstrates the usage of the `lastIndexOf` function by calling it with different arguments and logging the results to the console.\",\"/homeworks/hw32/scripts/app.js\":\"This file does not contain any classes. It contains the following functions:\\n\\n`initFormEvents`: Initializes the event listeners for the form.\\n\\n`initFreezeButton`: Initializes the event listener for the freeze button.\\n\\nThe file also imports variables from the `uiVariables.js` module and sets the initial content of various UI elements.\",\"/homeworks/hw27/README.md\":\"This document does not contain any code, but rather provides a set of functional and technical requirements for adding the ability to delete contacts from a list, with a confirmation modal. The key requirements are:\\n\\n1. Add a \\\"Delete\\\" or \\\"Remove\\\" button/icon to each contact in the UI.\\n2. Clicking the delete button should not immediately delete the contact, but instead open a confirmation modal.\\n3. The confirmation modal should have \\\"Confirm\\\" and \\\"Cancel\\\" buttons, and the contact\\u0027s name should be displayed in the modal.\\n4. Clicking \\\"Confirm\\\" should remove the contact from the state and the DOM, while \\\"Cancel\\\" should close the modal without deleting anything.\\n5. The contact\\u0027s unique ID should be used to identify the contact to be deleted, and the DOM elements should be linked to the contacts using data attributes.\\n6. Event delegation or individual event listeners should be used to handle the delete button clicks.\\n7. Additional features like toast notifications and closing the modal on backdrop click or Esc key press are suggested as optional.\",\"/homeworks/hw2/index.html\":\"This HTML file does not contain any classes or functions. It is a basic HTML document that includes a JavaScript file named \\\"script.js\\\" at the end of the `\\u003cbody\\u003e` section. The purpose of this file is to provide a structure for a web page and to load a JavaScript file that will likely contain the main functionality of the application.\",\"/homeworks/hw28/scripts/modules/formValidation.js\":\"This file does not contain any classes. It contains the following function:\\n\\n`validateForm`: This function takes in an array of input elements, an object of regular expressions for validation, and an object of error messages, and returns either the form data if the form is valid or `false` if the form is invalid.\\n\\nThe file exports the `validateForm` function as the default export.\",\"/homeworks/hw15/index.html\":\"This HTML file does not contain any classes or functions. It is a basic HTML document that includes a JavaScript file named \\\"script.js\\\" at the end of the `\\u003cbody\\u003e` section. The purpose of this file is to provide a structure for a web page and to load a JavaScript file that will likely contain the main functionality of the application.\",\"/homeworks/hw20/README.md\":\"This file does not contain any classes or functions. Instead, it presents four coding challenges that involve working with arrays and objects in JavaScript. The challenges cover the following tasks:\\n\\n1. `map + conditional transformation`: Transform an array of products into an array of strings in the format \\\"Name: $price\\\", with an additional \\\"(out of stock)\\\" suffix for products that are not in stock.\\n\\n2. `filter + multiple conditions`: Filter an array of users based on the following criteria: active, age between 18 and 35 (inclusive), and email not ending with \\\"@spam.com\\\".\\n\\n3. `reduce → grouping`: Group a set of transactions by their category and calculate the total amount for each category.\\n\\n4. `find + nested data search`: Find the first order that contains a product with the SKU \\\"B2\\\" and return the entire order.\\n\\nThe file provides the initial data structures and the expected output format for each challenge, allowing the reader to implement the solutions using JavaScript\\u0027s array methods and other programming techniques.\",\"/homeworks/hw33/README.md\":\"This file does not contain any classes or functions. Instead, it provides an overview of the `Object.seal()` and `Object.freeze()` methods in JavaScript, along with their key differences and use cases. The file also includes a meta section and a task to create an `user` object with specific requirements, such as accessor properties, property descriptors, and locking the object\\u0027s structure.\",\"/homeworks/hw5/README.md\":\"This file does not contain any classes or functions. Instead, it provides a simple scenario for checking access to a movie with an 18+ age restriction. The script prompts the user for their age, and based on the user\\u0027s input, it displays different messages:\\n\\n1. If the user cancels or does not enter any age, the script displays an alert saying \\\"Вік не вказано\\\" (Age not specified).\\n2. If the user is under 18, the script asks the user through a confirm dialog if they have an adult with them who can allow the viewing. If the user confirms, the script displays an alert saying \\\"Доступ дозволено з дозволу дорослого\\\" (Access allowed with adult permission). If the user cancels, the script displays an alert saying \\\"Доступ заборонено\\\" (Access denied).\\n3. If the user is 18 or older, the script displays an alert saying \\\"Доступ дозволено. Приємного перегляду!\\\" (Access allowed. Enjoy the movie!).\\n\\nThe script also ensures that the user\\u0027s age is converted to a number before the checks are performed, and it uses at least two if/else if statements to handle the different scenarios.\",\"/homeworks/hw34/index.html\":\"This file appears to be an HTML document that creates a simple note-taking application. It does not contain any defined classes or functions. Instead, it uses HTML, CSS, and JavaScript to create the user interface and functionality for the application. The CSS styles, which are imported from the Bootstrap library and a custom stylesheet.\\n3. The JavaScript code, which is imported from an external file and likely handles the logic for adding, marking, and deleting notes.\\n\\nThe file sets up the basic structure and styling for the note-taking application, and the JavaScript code (which is not provided in the given code snippet) likely implements the core functionality of the application.\",\"/homeworks/hw19/css/style.css\":\"There are no classes or functions in the provided document. Instead, it provides a set of tasks and requirements for creating and manipulating several objects, including `user`, `order`, and `systemSettings`. The main objectives are:\\n\\n1. Create the `user` object with specific properties, perform various operations on the object, and determine the user\\u0027s access to the service.\\n2. Create the `order` object with specific properties, perform various operations on the object, and build a textual description of the order.\\n3. Integrate the information from the three objects to determine the final access to the system, and display the appropriate message.\\n\\nThe file is focused on practicing object manipulation, conditional logic, and data integration in JavaScript.\",\"/homeworks/hw17/js/find.js\":\"This file does not contain any classes. It defines a single function, `find`, which returns the first element in the given array that satisfies the provided callback function, or `undefined` if no such element is found.\\n\\n`find`: This function takes an array and a callback function as arguments, and returns the first element in the array that satisfies the callback function, or `undefined` if no such element is found.\\n\\nThe file also demonstrates the usage of the `find` function by applying it to the `numbers` array with different callback functions.\",\"/homeworks/hw14/css/style.css\":\"There are no classes or functions in the provided file. The file appears to be an empty XML document with a `\\u003cdocument\\u003e` tag, but no content within it.\",\"/homeworks/hw17/js/every.js\":\"This file does not contain any classes. It defines a function called `every` that checks if all elements in an array satisfy a given callback function, and then demonstrates the usage of this function with some examples.\\n\\n`every`: A function that takes an array and a callback function, and returns `true` if all elements in the array satisfy the callback function, or `true` for an empty array.\\n\\nThe file demonstrates the usage of the `every` function with three examples:\\n1. Checking if all numbers in the `numbers` array are greater than 0.\\n2. Checking if all numbers in the `numbers` array are greater than 10.\\n3. Checking if all elements in an empty array are greater than 10.\",\"/homeworks/hw25/index.html\":\"This file does not contain any classes or functions. The document appears to be an empty XML document, which does not contain any meaningful content.\",\"/homeworks/hw32/README.md\":\"This file does not contain any classes or functions. Instead, it describes a task to create a web page with a profile model that can be saved, frozen, and displayed in a preview card. The key elements of the task are:\\n\\n1. Create a page with input fields for first name, last name, and email, along with \\\"Save\\\" and \\\"Freeze model\\\" buttons, an error block, and a profile preview card.\\n2. Implement a `profileModel` using `defineProperty` with private properties (`_firstName`, `_lastName`, `_email`) and public accessors (`firstName`, `lastName`, `email`) that validate the input.\\n3. Implement the \\\"Save\\\" functionality to update the profile model and the preview card, with error handling.\\n4. Implement the \\\"Freeze model\\\" functionality to prevent further updates to the model.\\n5. Manage the DOM elements, such as adding/removing the \\\"invalid\\\" class on input fields and rendering errors in the DOM.\\n6. (Bonus) Add a \\\"Show descriptors\\\" button that renders the JSON descriptors of the model in a `\\u003cpre\\u003e` element on the page.\",\"/homeworks/hw28/README.md\":\"This document does not contain any code, but rather provides instructions and guidelines for refactoring the JavaScript logic from a previous homework assignment (DZ 27) into separate modules, following the Single Responsibility Principle (SRP).\\n\\nThe key points are:\\n\\n1. Separate the JavaScript logic into individual modules, as per your own discretion.\\n2. Avoid the following anti-patterns:\\n - `ContactService` should not import the UI\\n - `Validation` should not know about the DOM, Bootstrap, or `listRenderer`\\n - The UI should not know about the `contactService`\\n - `uiToasts` should not trigger the `delete` function in `contactService`\\n\\nThe document also provides a link to an article explaining the SRP principle, and suggests using the ChatGPT tool to help explain how to properly separate the logic, but without generating any code.\",\"/homeworks/hw24/css/style.css\":\"This file contains CSS styles and does not have any classes or functions. The file defines two CSS classes:\\n\\n`.hidden`: This class sets the `display` property to `none`, effectively hiding the element.\\n\\n`.expanded`: This class sets the `display` property to `block`, making the element visible.\\n\\nThe purpose of this file is to provide CSS styles for toggling the visibility of elements on a web page, allowing them to be hidden or expanded as needed.\",\"/homeworks/hw28/scripts/modules/contactService.js\":\"This file does not contain any classes. It exports a module that provides a contacts management service with the following functions:\\n\\n`contactsManagement`: A function that creates and returns an object with methods for managing a list of contacts.\\n\\n`getContacts`: A function that returns a copy of the current list of contacts.\\n\\n`addContact`: A function that adds a new contact to the list of contacts.\\n\\n`removeContact`: A function that removes a contact from the list of contacts by its ID.\\n\\nThe file exports the `contactService` object, which is an instance of the `contactsManagement` function, allowing it to be used in other parts of the application.\",\"/homeworks/hw7/index.html\":\"This HTML file does not contain any classes or functions. The purpose of this file is to provide a basic HTML structure with a script tag that loads the \\\"indexOf.js\\\" file, which likely contains JavaScript code related to the `indexOf()` method or other array-related operations.\",\"/homeworks/hw28/scripts/app.js\":\"This file appears to be a JavaScript module that sets up the functionality for a contact management application. It imports various modules and functions, creates modals and toasts, and initializes the event handlers for the application. Here\\u0027s a summary of the classes and functions in the file:\\n\\n`contactService`: A module that provides functionality for managing contacts.\\n\\n`listHandler`: A module that handles the rendering of the contact list.\\n\\n`initEvents`: A function that initializes the event handlers for the application.\\n\\n`validateForm`: A function that validates the form input for adding a new contact.\\n\\n`createModal`: A function that creates a modal dialog with a customizable title, content, and footer.\\n\\n`createToast`: A function that creates a toast notification with a customizable title, body, and background class.\\n\\n`addContactModal`: An instance of the `createModal` function that creates a modal for adding a new contact.\\n\\n`deleteContactModal`: An instance of the `createModal` function that creates a modal for deleting a contact.\\n\\n`toastAdded`: An instance of the `createToast` function that creates a toast notification for when a contact is added.\\n\\n`toastDeleted`: An instance of the `createToast` function that creates a toast notification for when a contact is deleted.\",\"/homeworks/hw4/index.html\":\"This HTML file does not contain any classes or functions. The purpose of this file is to provide a structure for a web page and to load a JavaScript file that will likely contain the main functionality of the application.\",\"/homeworks/hw31/styles/style.css\":\"There is no code provided in the given input, so I cannot list the names of classes and functions or provide summaries for them. The file appears to be an empty XML document, which does not contain any classes or functions. Without any code to analyze, I can only summarize that this file is an empty XML document that does not perform any specific functionality.\",\"/homeworks/hw24/js/script.js\":\"This file does not contain any classes or functions. Instead, it sets up an event listener on an element with the ID \\u0027container\\u0027. When the user clicks on an element with the data attribute \\u0027data-readMoreBtn\\u0027, the code toggles the visibility of an element with the class \\u0027long\\u0027 within the closest \\u0027.card\\u0027 element. If the \\u0027.card\\u0027 element has the \\u0027expanded\\u0027 class, the \\u0027long\\u0027 element is hidden and the button text changes to \\u0027Read more\\u0027. If the \\u0027.card\\u0027 element does not have the \\u0027expanded\\u0027 class, the \\u0027long\\u0027 element is shown and the button text changes to \\u0027Hide\\u0027.\",\"/homeworks/hw26/js/script.js\":\"This file does not contain any classes. It contains the following functions:\\n\\n`addUserData`: Adds the user data to the DOM.\\n\\n`validateEmail`: Validates the email input field.\\n\\n`validatePassword`: Validates the password input field.\\n\\n`checkPasswordConfirm`: Checks if the password and confirm password fields match.\\n\\n`validateAge`: Validates the age input field.\\n\\n`validateCity`: Validates the city input field.\\n\\n`validateCheckbox`: Validates the checkbox input field.\\n\\n`validateInputs`: Validates all the input fields and returns an object of errors.\\n\\n`showErrors`: Displays the validation errors on the form.\\n\\n`window.addEventListener(\\u0027DOMContentLoaded\\u0027)`: Loads the user data from localStorage and populates the form.\\n\\n`allInputs.addEventListener(\\u0027focusout\\u0027)`: Performs live validation on each input field.\\n\\n`form.addEventListener(\\u0027submit\\u0027)`: Validates the form on submission, saves the data to localStorage, and updates the user data display.\\n\\n`document.getElementById(\\u0027reset-btn\\u0027).addEventListener(\\u0027click\\u0027)`: Clears the form and removes the user data from localStorage.\\n\\nThis file is a form validation script that handles the validation of various input fields, including email, password, age, city, and a checkbox. Creating a new array with the maximum value repeated.\\n\\nThe file demonstrates the use of various array methods and operations in JavaScript, such as `filter()`, `reduce()`, and `map()`.\",\"/homeworks/hw4/README.md\":\"This file does not contain any classes or functions. Instead, it describes a script that interacts with a user through a prompt and an alert. The script performs the following tasks:\\n\\n1. Calculates the discounted price of the product.\\n4. Displays an alert message with the initial price, the discount percentage, and the discounted price.\\n5. If the user enters invalid data, the script displays an \\\"Error\\\" alert message.\\n\\nThe script uses only primitive data types (number, string, boolean) and includes at least one `if` statement to validate the user\\u0027s input.\",\"/homeworks/hw13/js/script.js\":\"This file does not contain any classes or functions. Logs the final values of `sum` and `sqSum` to the console.\",\"/homeworks/hw13/README.md\":\"This file does not contain any classes or functions. It provides two code snippets and instructions for finding the sum of elements in an array and the sum of the squares of the elements in the array using a loop.\\n\\nThe first code snippet is:\\n\\n```js\\nconst arr \\u003d [1, 2, 3, 4, 5];\\n```\\n\\nThe instructions for this snippet are to find the sum of the elements in the array using a loop and output the result to the console.\\n\\nThe second code snippet is:\\n\\n```js\\nconst arr \\u003d [1, 2, 3, 4, 5];\\n```\\n\\nThe instructions for this snippet are to find the sum of the squares of the elements in the array using a loop. The document appears to be an empty XML document, which does not contain any meaningful content.\",\"/homeworks/hw5/js/script.js\":\"This file does not contain any classes or functions. Instead, it is a simple JavaScript code that prompts the user to enter their age and then checks if the user is eligible to access certain content based on their age. Here\\u0027s a summary of what the code does:\\n\\n1. The code prompts the user to enter their age using the `prompt()` function.\\n2. If the user does not enter an age or cancels the prompt, the code displays an alert message indicating that the age was not provided.\\n3. If the user enters a valid age (a number greater than or equal to 1), the code checks if the user is under 18 years old.\\n4. If the user is under 18, the code displays a confirmation dialog asking if the user has an adult with them who can provide permission to access the content.\\n5. If the user has an adult with them, the code displays an alert message indicating that access is granted with the adult\\u0027s permission.\\n6. If the user does not have an adult with them, the code displays an alert message indicating that access is denied.\\n7. If the user is 18 or older, the code displays an alert message indicating that access is granted.\",\"/homeworks/hw7/README.md\":\"This file does not contain any classes or functions. Instead, it provides a programming task and a parsing exercise.\\n\\nThe programming task involves:\\n1. Prompting the user for their age using the `prompt()` function.\\n2. Displaying an alert message if the user\\u0027s input is not a valid number.\\n5. Calculating the user\\u0027s age in 5 years and displaying a message.\\n\\nThe parsing exercise involves:\\n1. Parsing numbers from different string formats using `parseInt()` and `parseFloat()`.\\n2. Demonstrating the differences between `Number()`, `parseInt()`, and `parseFloat()` when dealing with \\\"dirty\\\" strings.\\n3. Explaining why `parseInt()` and `parseFloat()` return a number or `NaN` for certain input strings.\",\"/homeworks/hw34/scripts/modules/controller/Controller.js\":\"This file defines a `Controller` Finding the largest element and setting the rest to zero.\",\"/homeworks/hw31/README.md\":\"This file defines a `Student` class with the following properties and methods:\\n\\n`Student`: A class that represents a student with properties for name, surname, birth year, grades, and attendance, and methods to calculate age, average grade, and provide a summary of the student\\u0027s performance.\\n\\n`constructor`: Initializes a new `Student` object with the provided name, surname, birth year, and grades.\\n\\n`age`: Calculates the student\\u0027s age based on their birth year.\\n\\n`averageGrade`: Calculates the student\\u0027s average grade based on the grades array.\\n\\n`present`: Marks the student as present in the attendance array.\\n\\n`absent`: Marks the student as absent in the attendance array.\\n\\n`summary`: Provides a summary of the student\\u0027s performance based on their average grade and attendance rate.\\n\\nThe file also demonstrates the usage of the `Student` class by creating 2-3 student instances and calling the various methods.\",\"/homeworks/hw8/js/script1.js\":\"This file does not contain any classes or functions. Instead, it demonstrates the use of object destructuring in JavaScript. The file creates an object `user` with various properties, and then uses object destructuring to extract the values of those properties into separate variables. It then creates a new object `shortInfo` that contains only the `name` and `city` properties from the original `user` object. Finally, it creates another object `renamed` that renames the properties of `shortInfo` to `fullName` and `location`. The file then logs the various variables and objects to the console.\"}"}
